text:
Under the vast canvas of an amber moon, the old oak tree whispered secrets on the wind. Inside its gnarled branches curled a goldenrod, its petals catching the moonlight. That night, a lone fox sought shelter beneath the tree.

Foxes don't venture far from home at night. Their world revolves around familiarity, comfort. But not tonight's traveler.

The fox' keen senses discerned more than just the shelter. A sense of adventure pulsed through its veins. It leaned closer to the goldenrod.

With a quick tug from its mouthful of fur—

The fox was no longer alone.

A figure loomed beside it, its silhouette large against the twinkling stars. Its eyes glowed a peculiar shade of lilac.

"I take it you're hungry," the figure spoke, voice deep like the wellspring that fed the forest.

The fox nodded eagerly. Its tail twitched indignantly.

"Oh good," the figure continued. "That'll solve your problem."
